event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Kanata Rangers vs Ennismore Eagles (Game #143)
event_log.detail: We need a strong start against Ennismore Eagles as we begin our quest to win Pool C at the OWHA U15B Provincials.

event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Kincardine Kinucks vs Windsor Wildcats (Game #142)
event_log.detail: We need to closely watch this matchup since both Kincardine and Windsor are direct competitors for first in Pool C, and the result will impact our path to winning the pool.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kanata Rangers 1 - 0 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: If we hold this 1-0 lead against Ennismore Eagles, we'll be in a strong position to win Pool C, taking first place in 106 of 214 scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kanata Rangers 1 - 1 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: A tie here would put us in a strong position to win Pool C, as we'd claim first place in 38 of 225 scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kincardine Kinucks 1 - 0 Windsor Wildcats (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result puts us in a stronger position as we would win the pool in 48 of 214 scenarios if the score holds.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Kanata Rangers 1 - 1 Ennismore Eagles
event_log.detail: This was an upset - we tied against the higher-ranked #25 Ennismore Eagles despite being the stronger #18 team! The tie significantly hurt our pool chances, as we now win Pool C in only 38 out of 243 remaining outcome combinations, down from 156 out of 729 before the game.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kincardine Kinucks 2 - 0 Windsor Wildcats (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result puts us in a stronger position to win Pool C, as we would win the pool in 12 of 77 scenarios if the score holds.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Kincardine Kinucks 2 - 0 Windsor Wildcats
event_log.detail: The #3 ranked Kincardine Kinucks defeated #41 Windsor as expected, putting them in first place with 2 points while we remain tied for second with Ennismore at 1 point each. This result hurt our pool chances significantly - we now win Pool C in only 12 out of the remaining 81 outcome combinations, down from 38 out of 243 before this game.

event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Ennismore Eagles vs Kincardine Kinucks (Game #163)
event_log.detail: We need Ennismore Eagles to win because it would prevent Kincardine Kinucks from extending their lead at the top of Pool C.

event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Windsor Wildcats vs Kanata Rangers (Game #162)
event_log.detail: We need to secure points in this matchup to stay in contention for first in Pool C with the Kincardine Kinucks currently leading at 2 points.

event_log.headline: Score update: Windsor Wildcats 1 - 0 Kanata Rangers (in progress)
event_log.detail: If this score holds, we would be eliminated from contention to win Pool C, as we'd win the pool in 0 of the 27 scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Windsor Wildcats 2 - 0 Kanata Rangers (in progress)
event_log.detail: If this score holds, we would be eliminated from contention to win Pool C, as we win the pool in 0 of 27 scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Ennismore Eagles 0 - 1 Kincardine Kinucks (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result puts us in a very challenging position, as we would win the pool in only 3 of 27 scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Windsor Wildcats 2 - 1 Kanata Rangers (in progress)
event_log.detail: If this score holds, we would be eliminated from contention to win Pool C, as we win the pool in 0 of the 27 possible scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Ennismore Eagles 0 - 2 Kincardine Kinucks (in progress)
event_log.detail: With Ennismore losing, we would need to beat Kincardine in our final game to have a realistic chance at first in Pool C.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Windsor Wildcats 2 - 1 Kanata Rangers
event_log.detail: What a heartbreaking result for us - we've been completely eliminated from Pool C after falling 2-1 to the much lower-ranked Windsor Wildcats in a major upset. Our elimination means we can no longer win the pool in any of the 27 remaining outcome combinations, ending our championship hopes despite being the #18 ranked team facing #41 Windsor.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Ennismore Eagles 0 - 2 Kincardine Kinucks
event_log.detail: This wasn't an upset - higher-ranked Kincardine (#3) defeated lower-ranked Ennismore (#25) as expected. Unfortunately, this result has officially eliminated us from Pool C contention, as we can no longer win the pool in any of the 9 remaining outcome combinations.

event_log.headline: Pool F: Durham West Lightning 0 - 1 London Devilettes (Final)
event_log.detail: QF opponent pool update.

event_log.headline: Pool F: North Halton Twisters 0 - 2 Waterloo Ravens (Final)

event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Windsor Wildcats vs Ennismore Eagles (Game #701)
event_log.detail: We need Windsor to win this game to give us the best chance of winning Pool C, as it would prevent Ennismore from gaining points that could complicate our path to first place.

event_log.headline: Score update: Windsor Wildcats 1 - 0 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result would eliminate our chances to win Pool C, as we would need other results to go our way that are no longer possible.

scouting.description: This season we've faced several teams heading to Provincials across 5 tournaments. We swept St. Catharines Brock Jr. Badgers in two meetings (6-0, 2-0). Against the rest... let's just say we're looking for redemption. Of note: Durham West Lightning (#29) is in our quarterfinal opponent's pool (Pool F) -- they beat us 2-0 in November, but they're not the favourite to win their pool. In tournament play, anything can happen!

_narrative:
**Where We Stand**
Earlier we predicted Kincardine would extend their lead over Ennismore, and that's exactly what happened with their 2-0 victory. We remain tied for third in Pool C with 1 point alongside Ennismore, while Kincardine has now pulled away as the clear pool leader with a perfect 4 points from two wins.

**Our Next Game**
As anticipated in our last update, we face the pool-leading Kincardine Kinucks tomorrow morning at 10:30 AM on Saturday April 11th. The 3rd-ranked Kinucks have lived up to their billing with two shutout victories, outscoring their opponents 4-0 through their first two games.

**Around the Pool**
With Kincardine's latest win confirmed, the pool dynamics remain exactly as we projected – our scenario analysis continues to show we win the pool in 0 out of 9 possible combinations. Windsor and Ennismore will battle simultaneously at 10:30 AM, with both teams still in contention for second place in the pool.

_coaches_corner.don_cherry: I'll tell ya, these Kanata Rangers girls are showing real heart out there - a tie with Ennismore and battling hard against Windsor shows they got that never-quit spirit! Now they got the Kincardine Kinucks next and that's where champions are made, kids!
_coaches_corner.talking_points:
**Mindset**
That loss to Windsor stings, but we showed real character in how we battled back in the third period. Remember how strong we looked against Ennismore when we controlled the pace and created chances - that's the team we are when we play our game.

**The Opponent**
Kincardine is ranked #3 in the province for a reason - they're skilled, disciplined, and they'll capitalize on our mistakes. They've been here before and know how to handle pressure situations, so we need to match their intensity from the opening faceoff.

**What We Need**
Mathematically we're facing long odds, but hockey isn't played on paper - we need a win and help from other games to have any chance at advancing. Play smart, stay out of the penalty box, and make every shift count because this could be our last game of the season.

**Our Game Plan**
Focus on the systems that got us here - support the puck, finish our checks, and get traffic in front of their goalie. We've got nothing to lose now, so play with confidence and leave everything on the ice.

_tb_health:
**Tiebreaker Health Analysis:**

We're currently tied with Windsor on both goal differential (-1) and goals against (3), meaning any tie with them goes to the next tiebreaker rule. We're behind Ennismore on both metrics (they have 0 GD vs our -1, and 1 GA vs our 3), so we'd lose head-to-head tiebreakers against them.

Our position is extremely fragile - even one additional goal allowed puts us further behind on GA, and we have no cushion on goal differential. Since we're already at the bottom of the tiebreaker hierarchy among tied teams, every goal against us matters significantly.

We need to focus on defensive play and goal differential management, as we cannot afford to fall further behind on either metric in our remaining games.
